title: acaena novae-zelandiae
intro: Acaena novae-zelandiae, also known as the New Zealand bugseed or sheep's bur, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. In herbal preparations, Acaena novae-zelandiae is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its uses and effectiveness may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual needs.
